---
title: "06 · Fine-tuning fundamentals — LoRA"
description: "Planned · When to fine-tune, when to prompt. LoRA math + QLoRA, run on a small open model end-to-end."
author: "Prerna Tyagi"
date: "2026-05-28"
categories: [planned, fine-tuning, lora]
reading-time: true
toc: false
---

<p class="breadcrumb-mono">[← series](../../)</p>

::: {.callout-tip appearance="simple"}
**Planned.** First application post in *Building Blocks of Modern AI* — builds directly on attention ([04](../04-attention-from-scratch/)) and optimization ([05](../05-loss-optimization-backprop/)).
:::

## Planned coverage

- The fine-tuning question — when does it beat a well-engineered prompt
- **Full fine-tuning** — what it costs (compute, memory, dataset, eval discipline)
- **LoRA** — the low-rank decomposition math, why r=8 is a workable default
- **QLoRA** — what 4-bit quantization buys you in memory
- Practical workflow on a small open model (TinyLlama or similar) — end to end
- Eval-driven iteration — picking when to stop training
